initial_program:
% Grid dimensions are implicit via input facts.
row(R) :- input(R, _, _).
col(C) :- input(_, C, _).

% Leftmost nonzero cell of each row (if any)
leftmost(R, Lcol, V) :-
    input(R, Lcol, V),
    color(V),
    V != 0,
    col(Lcol),
    Lcol = #min{ C : input(R, C, D), color(D), D != 0 }.

% Does the row contain a nonzero cell
row_has_nonzero(R) :- leftmost(R, Lcol, V).

% Build alternating pattern starting at the leftmost cell.
expectedState(R, Lcol, 0) :- leftmost(R, Lcol, V).
expectedState(R, Cnext, 1) :- expectedState(R, Cprev, 0),
                              col(Cprev), col(Cnext), Cnext = Cprev + 1.
expectedState(R, Cnext, 0) :- expectedState(R, Cprev, 1),
                              col(Cprev), col(Cnext), Cnext = Cprev + 1.

% Output according to the pattern.
output(R, C, V) :- expectedState(R, C, 0), leftmost(R, Lcol, V), col(C).
output(R, C, 5) :- expectedState(R, C, 1), leftmost(R, Lcol, V), col(C).

% Cells left of the leftmost cell are zero.
output(R, C, 0) :- leftmost(R, Lcol, V), col(C), C < Lcol.

% Rows without any nonzero cell are all zero.
output(R, C, 0) :- not row_has_nonzero(R), col(C).

% Exactly one colour per cell.
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- row(R), col(C).

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Grid dimensions are implicit via input facts.
row(R) :- input(R, _, _).
col(C) :- input(_, C, _).

% Leftmost nonzero cell of each row (if any)
leftmost(R, Lcol, V) :-
    input(R, Lcol, V),
    color(V),
    V != 0,
    col(Lcol),
    Lcol = #min{ C : input(R, C, D), color(D), D != 0 }.

% Does the row contain a nonzero cell
row_has_nonzero(R) :- leftmost(R, Lcol, V).

% Build alternating pattern starting at the leftmost cell.
expectedState(R, Lcol, 0) :- leftmost(R, Lcol, V).
expectedState(R, Cnext, 1) :- expectedState(R, Cprev, 0),
                              col(Cprev), col(Cnext), Cnext = Cprev + 1.
expectedState(R, Cnext, 0) :- expectedState(R, Cprev, 1),
                              col(Cprev), col(Cnext), Cnext = Cprev + 1.

% Output according to the pattern.
output(R, C, V) :- expectedState(R, C, 0), leftmost(R, Lcol, V), col(C).
output(R, C, 5) :- expectedState(R, C, 1), leftmost(R, Lcol, V), col(C).

% Cells left of the leftmost cell are zero.
output(R, C, 0) :- leftmost(R, Lcol, V), col(C), C < Lcol.

% Rows without any nonzero cell are all zero.
output(R, C, 0) :- row(R), not row_has_nonzero(R), col(C).

% Exactly one colour per cell.
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- row(R), col(C).

#show output/3.

final_program:
% Grid dimensions are implicit via input facts.
row(R) :- input(R, _, _).
col(C) :- input(_, C, _).

% Leftmost nonzero cell of each row (if any)
leftmost(R, Lcol, V) :-
    input(R, Lcol, V),
    color(V),
    V != 0,
    col(Lcol),
    Lcol = #min{ C : input(R, C, D), color(D), D != 0 }.

% Does the row contain a nonzero cell
row_has_nonzero(R) :- leftmost(R, Lcol, V).

% Build alternating pattern starting at the leftmost cell.
expectedState(R, Lcol, 0) :- leftmost(R, Lcol, V).
expectedState(R, Cnext, 1) :- expectedState(R, Cprev, 0),
                              col(Cprev), col(Cnext), Cnext = Cprev + 1.
expectedState(R, Cnext, 0) :- expectedState(R, Cprev, 1),
                              col(Cprev), col(Cnext), Cnext = Cprev + 1.

% Output according to the pattern.
output(R, C, V) :- expectedState(R, C, 0), leftmost(R, Lcol, V), col(C).
output(R, C, 5) :- expectedState(R, C, 1), leftmost(R, Lcol, V), col(C).

% Cells left of the leftmost cell are zero.
output(R, C, 0) :- leftmost(R, Lcol, V), col(C), C < Lcol.

% Rows without any nonzero cell are all zero.
output(R, C, 0) :- row(R), not row_has_nonzero(R), col(C).

% Exactly one colour per cell.
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- row(R), col(C).

#show output/3.
